verses.text: 'When Israel was a child, then I loved him, and called my son out of Egypt'; yet the more He called, the more they strayed. A father's love. God's tender care met with constant turning away.
'When Israel was a child, then I loved him, and called my son out of Egypt'; yet the more He called, the more they strayed. A father's love. God's tender care met with constant turning away.
'When Israel was a child, then I loved him, and called my son out of Egypt'; yet the more He called, the more they strayed. A father's love. God's tender care met with constant turning away.
He drew them 'with bands of love,' easing the yoke and feeding them; but they refused to return. Gentle leading. The kindness of God is spurned by a wandering heart.
He drew them 'with bands of love,' easing the yoke and feeding them; but they refused to return. Gentle leading. The kindness of God is spurned by a wandering heart.
He drew them 'with bands of love,' easing the yoke and feeding them; but they refused to return. Gentle leading. The kindness of God is spurned by a wandering heart.

Though they are 'bent to backsliding,' God cries, 'How shall I give thee up, Ephraim?' His heart is turned, and He will not execute His fierce anger. Mercy wrestling with wrath. Love restrains the judgment sin deserves.

Though they are 'bent to backsliding,' God cries, 'How shall I give thee up, Ephraim?' His heart is turned, and He will not execute His fierce anger. Mercy wrestling with wrath. Love restrains the judgment sin deserves.

Though they are 'bent to backsliding,' God cries, 'How shall I give thee up, Ephraim?' His heart is turned, and He will not execute His fierce anger. Mercy wrestling with wrath. Love restrains the judgment sin deserves.
They shall follow the LORD when He roars like a lion, and His children shall come trembling home from the west. Regathered. The voice that judges also calls the exiles back.
